case (i.e. danger from ascetics); the word Pdyu (anus) means
Upastha [goniia.l organ) ) Avalokayan ik$an (for xkiamdnah). Brahma-
varcah, Sragddmah, Ratnabheddni etc. are some other instances. In
some places we have taken a different view from that of Uipala. For
example he says: 'arrtr^r: sr?f^r which is against the views of
the ancients.
He ridicules the pseudo-astronomers who speak of the sun's
victory and'defeat in planetary war.
Utpala's commentary was abridged, we are told, under the
name, Utpalaparimala by one Bhdskara son of Kumdra, grandson of
i
Rama otNatdhruva Kdiyapa golra and Vdryaganya family. This is not
available in print.
1. VideLXXV. 7.

xlviii Brhat Samhita
The commentator speaks of some of the verses of the text as
Andr$a i.e- not based on ancient authority, indicating thereby that
they are not genuine : They are XVII. 9,10; XXV. 6 and XXVIII.
17, 23 and 24. He has not commented either on the last three
verses.
